The Climb of Amlenk

Y11S1

4,525 RP (Champion)

Amlenk's seasonal campaign represents a highly structured effort in competitive ranking. Across a deep set of matches, they have operated as a critical force in the competitive hierarchy of their team.

Amlenk's season represents a masterclass in raw mechanical execution and flex anchoring. In the elite lobbies of Ranked 2.0, mechanical supremacy alone hits a ceiling. An executive tactical analysis reveals a critical bottleneck: while Amlenk's defensive anchoring is near-impenetrable, their offensive executes are frequently plagued by stalled momentum, slow utility clears, and late-round post-plant collapses.

Season Objective

Systematically bridge the tactical execution gap to achieve a dominant 58.0%+ win rate and secure top-100 Champion status.

Tactical Performance Profile

Pro-League Analytical Verdict

The seasonal win rate of 48.7% represents an exact delta of -3.1% compared to lifetime performance, confirming that individual combat mechanics (K/D: 1.35) must be integrated into team support systems. In Ranked 2.0, round conversion is the ultimate metric of success.

Map Winrate Overview

Attack vs Defence Win%

Map Deep Dive

Comfort Zones (Top 3 Comfort Maps)

Lair (22 matches | 59.1% Win% | 1.45 K/D): Balanced on both sides with only a 9.7% attack-defence gap, meaning round wins aren't side-dependent. K/D of 1.45 tracks with your overall map average, so wins are coming from positioning, not just raw frags. Only 22 matches — promising numbers, but the sample is still building.
Kafe Dostoyevsky (60 matches | 58.3% Win% | 1.55 K/D): Defence is the engine here — 61.0% def WR carries the overall number while attack sits at just 43.7%. K/D of 1.55 is well above the 1.33 map average — gunfights here consistently go your way. With 60 matches this is one of your most-played maps, so the data is rock solid.
Bank (36 matches | 58.3% Win% | 1.33 K/D): Defensive anchoring is dialled in at 61.7%, providing a reliable foundation that absorbs lost attack rounds. Interestingly, K/D (1.33) is slightly below your 1.33 average — wins come from smart site holds rather than kill volume. 36 matches gives a solid read on this map.

Struggle Zones (Bottom 3 Struggle Maps)

Theme Park (9 matches | 33.3% Win% | 1.46 K/D): Win rate stuck at 33.3% despite a playable 56.7% defence — attack rounds are being given away. K/D of 1.46 is actually strong — you're winning gunfights but losing rounds, which screams poor timing or trade structure. ESR of 0.44 confirms early-round struggles — opening engagements are going south too often.
Outback (9 matches | 22.2% Win% | 1.14 K/D): Both sides are bleeding: 38.7% on attack and 48.4% on defence. This isn't a one-side fix — site selection and anchor positioning are both off. K/D of 1.14 is breakeven, so the issue isn't aim — it's how rounds are being closed out. ESR of 0.30 confirms early-round struggles — opening engagements are going south too often.
Emerald Plains (1 matches | 0.0% Win% | 1.25 K/D): Only 1 match played — this could be noise rather than a real weakness. The 0% win rate is alarming on paper but statistically meaningless at this sample size.

Attack vs Defence Asymmetry Rules

Implement these map-specific tactical corrections immediately to bridge the offense-defense discrepancy:

Skyscraper (23.6% Att vs 64.9% Def WR — 41.3% Gap): The 23.6% attack rate means entries through tea room and geisha are being shut down hard. Run exhibition hall wrap-arounds and force defenders to deal with cross-angles they can't hold simultaneously.
Villa (36.6% Att vs 62.1% Def WR — 25.5% Gap): The 2F study/aviator default is heavily pre-aimed by defenders. Instead of dry-peeking long angles, prioritise vertical play from above through astro hatch and library ceiling to displace anchors before committing to site.
Clubhouse (34.7% Att vs 56.5% Def WR — 21.8% Gap): CCTV and cash room pushes fail when vertical pressure isn't established first. Take gym control and open hatches before any horizontal push — defenders lose half their angles when the floor is gone.
Fortress (38.3% Att vs 59.4% Def WR — 21.1% Gap): The sprawling layout means entries get fragmented. Stack your push through commando and maintain tight spacing — don't let the team get split across three different wings.
Chalet (41.5% Att vs 61.1% Def WR — 19.6% Gap): Garage-focused attacks are getting read. Mix in kitchen/trophy executes to keep defenders from stacking utility on one side. Drone the wine cellar rotation before committing bodies.

Operator Coaching Engine

Azami Defender

265 Rounds
K/D Ratio
1.36
Win Rate
60.4%

PROS: Elite space management with 265 rounds played, maintaining an outstanding K/D of 1.36 and a win rate of 60.4%.

FOCUS AREAS: Over-committing to visual barricades in pre-cleared lanes. Ensure your Kiba barriers are deployed dynamically in late-round trades.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Skyscraper, deploy Kiba barriers on tea room rotations to establish bulletproof head-holes while protecting anchors from terrace sightlines.

Aruni Defender

235 Rounds
K/D Ratio
1.61
Win Rate
53.2%

PROS: Excellent laser gate area denial with 235 rounds played, maintaining a K/D of 1.61 and 53.2% win rate.

FOCUS AREAS: Failing to reset Surya Gates after they've been deactivated. Always punch or throw trash utility to reactivate them once the cooldown finishes.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Clubhouse, place Surya gates on the main dirt tunnel entrance to block quick basement entries and force attackers to waste utility.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Oregon, hide Razorblooms under white stairs landing, forcing attackers to swing blindly into lethal crossfires.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Oregon, block laundry stairs completely during prep phase using tight stairwell jammer setups to keep defensive layouts hidden.

Priority Improvement Matrix

Focus Area Current State Target State Strategic Rationale
1. Entry Success Rate (ESR) Optimization Average ESR: 0.61 across Ranked map pool. Achieve a target ESR of 0.69+ through active pre-drone entry. Winning the opening duel increases the team round win probability by over 30% in competitive lobbies.
2. Attack Execute Conversion Sub-optimal round wins on comfort maps due to slow executes. Secure attack win rates above 48% across all 17 competitive maps. Accelerating structural breaches prevents defenders from using late-round denial utility (e.g. Smoke/Tubarão).
3. Operator Selection Discipline Flexing to lower-value operators on key sites. Restrict operator pool to top-performing stars (Azami, Thorn, Mute, Thermite). Committing to high-success index operators maximizes background round utility and gunfight effectiveness.
4. Flank & Intel Denial Vulnerable to roamer pinches on deep executes. Deploy 100% reliable flank watch using Nomad Airjabs and Gridlock Trax. Blocking defender rotations allows the main entry stack to concentrate fully on site clears.
5. Offense-Defense Symmetry Significant win rate variance between Attack and Defense. Achieve less than 12% win rate variance between attack and defense sides. Climbs in Ranked 2.0 depend heavily on winning rounds on both sides, avoiding uncoordinated clutch scenarios.

Ban & Veto Strategy (Y11S1 — Ranked 2.0)

Top 2 Maps to Ban

Emerald Plains

0.0% WR | 1.25 K/D. Statistically the poorest map. Uncoordinated vertical sweeps lead to structural site defeats. Ban immediately in drafting phase.

Outback

22.2% WR | 1.14 K/D. Highly dangerous terrain where uncoordinated attacks result in early breacher deaths. Deny from the map draft pool.

Top 2 Maps to Protect

Lair

59.1% WR | 1.45 K/D. Premier map comfort. Exceptional horizontal spacing and trade execution. Pick immediately if left open.

Kafe Dostoyevsky

58.3% WR | 1.55 K/D. Flawless site anchor layouts and high defensive win rates. Protect and leverage established angles.

Trio Stack Synergy & climbing Tips

As the mechanical powerhouse and Champion MMR of the stack, Amlenk's primary role is flex-anchor and late-round trade cleanups. Coordinate closely with WamaiDoingThis's utility: anchor alongside his Mute jammers, and trade him out if attackers attempt to flood laundry or church stairs on Oregon. On attack, let Covetous_Demon create early space as Ash, while Amlenk follows as flex/support (Ace/Twitch) to execute the plant. Ensure WamaiDoingThis's flank watch is secure before committing to site swings.

Veto Watchlist

Bank (56.1% WR) and Border (54.9% WR) are situational comfort zones. High individual combat effectiveness is maintained here, but round wins are lost during uncoordinated entries. Only pick if your stack has dedicated support roles pre-assigned.

3 Actionable Climbing Tips

1. Hidden MMR Coordinated Queueing

Pre-stack with your trio instead of solo-queuing, which heavily penalizes individual MMR in Ranked 2.0 matchmaking systems.

2. Utility-First Attack Executes

Establish drone routes to clear defender utility before the 1:15 mark, securing safe entries.

3. Breach-Denial Tricking

Coordinate active Jäger/Wamai interceptors to protect Bandit/Kaid wall denial from vertical hatches.
